title: Summer STEM Events — US & Global
intro: An interactive calendar of the biggest summer STEM happenings — robotics championships, rocket launches, air shows, AI conferences, and hackathons — across the United States and around the world. Click any highlighted day to see what's on.
disclaimer: Dates, venues, and eligibility change every year. This calendar is for discovery only — always confirm details on each event's official website before making plans or traveling.

events.name: CVPR 2026 — Computer Vision & Pattern Recognition
events.summary: The world's top computer-vision conference, where breakthroughs in AI image recognition, robotics perception, and generative models are unveiled.

events.name: International Space Development Conference
events.summary: Astronauts, engineers, and space entrepreneurs gather to plan humanity's future off-world, with a dedicated youth and educator track.

events.name: Regeneron ISEF Alumni Showcase
events.summary: A free online showcase of award-winning student research projects from the world's largest pre-college science competition.

events.name: RoboCup 2026 World Championship
events.summary: Thousands of robots from 40+ countries compete in soccer, rescue, and home-assistance leagues — including RoboCupJunior for school students.

events.name: Space Camp Summer Sessions
events.summary: Week-long astronaut-training camps running all summer: simulated missions, rocket builds, and the famous multi-axis trainer.

events.name: ICML 2026 — International Conference on Machine Learning
events.summary: One of the two most important machine-learning conferences in the world, where the next generation of AI research is presented.

events.name: Farnborough International Airshow
events.summary: One of the largest aerospace trade shows on Earth — jet demos, space tech, and a student 'Futures Day' aimed at inspiring young engineers.

events.name: EAA AirVenture Oshkosh
events.summary: 'The World's Greatest Aviation Celebration' — 10,000 aircraft, air shows, astronauts, and a hands-on KidVenture area for young aviators.

events.name: MLH Global Hack Week: Summer
events.summary: A free week-long online hackathon with daily mini-challenges, workshops, and prizes — perfect for first-time coders.

events.name: MIT Beaver Works Summer Institute Finals
events.summary: The finale of MIT's elite summer institute, where teams race autonomous vehicles and demo AI and robotics capstone projects.

events.name: SmallSat Conference
events.summary: The world's premier small-satellite conference — CubeSats, launch startups, and the student engineers building them.

events.name: DEF CON 34
events.audience: All ages · r00tz Asylum for kids
events.summary: The world's largest hacker convention, featuring 'r00tz Asylum' — a dedicated ethical-hacking village that teaches kids cybersecurity.

events.name: SIGGRAPH 2026 — Computer Graphics & Interactive Tech
events.summary: Where movie VFX, real-time graphics, and generative-AI art collide — plus a huge student volunteer and research program.

events.name: Perseid Meteor Shower — Peak Night
events.summary: The year's most beloved meteor shower peaks overnight — up to 100 shooting stars an hour. Grab a blanket and look up.

events.name: World Robot Conference
events.summary: A massive showcase of humanoid robots, industrial automation, and an international youth robotics tournament.

events.name: World Robot Olympiad — Season Kickoff
events.summary: Regional qualifiers begin for the global student robotics olympiad, leading to an international final in the fall.

events.name: NASA International Space Apps — Summer Bootcamps
events.summary: Warm-up bootcamps for the world's largest global hackathon, using real NASA open data to solve space and Earth challenges.

events.name: AI4ALL Summer Programs Demo Day
events.summary: Students who spent the summer learning AI present projects for social good, from healthcare to climate.

events.name: Crewed & Cargo Launch Window (Watch Party)
events.summary: A summer launch window from the Space Coast — watch in person from the visitor complex or on NASA's free livestream.

events.name: FIRST Robotics Off-Season Championships
events.summary: Regional off-season tournaments let rookie and veteran FIRST teams practice, recruit, and celebrate before the new game drops.

events.name: Global Drone & UAV Youth Challenge
events.summary: Students design flight paths and pilot drones through timed courses in a friendly international competition.

events.name: HackMIT Blueprint — Beginner Hackathon
events.summary: A weekend hackathon and learn-a-thon built specifically for high-school students taking their first steps into coding.
